subject: [A1POLICYMANAGEMENT] Guilin version bump
commit_message:
[A1POLICYMANAGEMENT] Guilin version bump

Bump image versions to use Guilin M4 version of A1 Policy Management image

Issue-ID: CCSDK-2854
Change-Id: Iee58442b0cd56c1a3e68a96d4ce62c32e810f90a
Signed-off-by: Lathish <lathishbabu.ganesan@est.tech>

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

two things:

* your pod don't have limits and it should be (see nbi as an example for limits)
* the commit message (on OOM) must follow this form:

[NAME_OF_COMPONENT|DOC|COMMON|GENERIC] Meaningful title (from OOM side)

at least one sentence explaining the change done in this patch, cause and consequences and possibly more of course

Issue-ID: AS_WE_ARE_FORCED_BUT_MEANINGLESS
Change-ID: xxx
Sign-off: xxx

Commit message will be the last stuff that will stay with our code so it must clearly explain the changes, the "why" and the consequences. If it change OOM behavior in any way, documentation must be also updated.

Starting Frankfurt branching, merge requests which are not following this pattern will not be merged.

Please read the following pages and follow the guidelines for writing commit message contained therein.

    http://bit.ly/goodcommitmessages
    http://who-t.blogspot.com/2009/12/on-commit-messages.html
    http://dep.debian.net/deps/dep3/
Uploaded patch set 2: Commit message was updated.

Patch Set 2:

Updated the Commit Message.
Regarding pod limits do you mean the below configuration in values file? If so it's already there. Or am I missing something?

flavor: small
# Segregation for Different environment (Small and Large)
resources:
  small:
    limits:
      cpu: 1
      memory: 2Gi
    requests:
      cpu: 100m
      memory: 1Gi
  large:
    limits:
      cpu: 2
      memory: 4Gi
    requests:
      cpu: 200m
      memory: 2Gi
  unlimited: {}

Patch Set 2:

three things worth to note here:

the good:

* you're making "a lot" of releases. This a GOOD pattern, don't hesitate, we (OOM and Integration teams) are fully supportive 😊 
 
what can be improved:

* A1 Policy Management is a new feature of ONAP but I (and so a lot of people also I guess) don't really know what it's. I believe you'll have a section in onap doc so I'd like to have a link somewhere in OOM
* I'm always uncomfortable when we have services with no e2e tests using them. Do you plan to have some that can be given to integration team?

> * I'm always uncomfortable when we have services with no e2e tests using them. Do you plan to have some that can be given to integration team?


Thank you Sylvain for your comments.

We are preparing our docs and it will be available soon. I will share this with you when it's ready.
We have some CSIT test's for this component. 
https://gerrit.onap.org/r/gitweb?p=integration/csit.git;a=tree;f=plans/ccsdk-oran/polmansuite;h=c5bd10cbf1feab496d9f0a47cc4e9b9ea3e06a39;hb=refs/heads/master

Patch Set 2:

I agree with Sylvain. We must also provide some documentation on this new component.
The requirements for memory are important. Why do we need 2Go RAM ?

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> I agree with Sylvain. We must also provide some documentation on this new component.
> The requirements for memory are important. Why do we need 2Go RAM ?

Hello Eric,
This component is migrated from O-RAN & we haven't configured any memory limits there. So this limits are some initial values & may vary during future releases.

Patch Set 2:

I'll submit and will update the requests/ limits value during the week.
I expect links from OOM to A1 doc ASAP or I'll have to revert the patches correlated to A1 Policy Management 

(as said in https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/OOM+Guilin+release+plan: If it change OOM behavior in any way, documentation must be also updated.)
Change has been successfully merged by Sylvain Desbureaux
